name: Hristo Borisov
title: Co-Founder & CEO
stage: Series B (extended to $215M; unicorn at $1B valuation since March 2022; new fundraise discussions ongoing as of early 2026)
hq_location: Sofia, Bulgaria (operational HQ); London, UK (registered HQ per PitchBook)
summary: Hristo Borisov is the Co-Founder and CEO of Payhawk, Bulgaria's first unicorn, which he built into a $1B+ corporate spend management platform operating across 32+ countries in Europe and the US.
suggested_action: Payhawk is a unicorn-stage company — well beyond the fund's investment scope. Borisov is an active angel investor in at least 8 startups and a prominent figure in the Bulgarian and broader CEE ecosystem. Reach out to position him as an LP, scout/dealflow source, or advisor; specifically, ask for warm introductions to early-stage CEE fintech or B2B SaaS founders in his network. Note also that Payhawk is actively seeking to acquire early-stage Series A startups — flagging relevant portfolio companies to Borisov could open a reciprocal dealflow relationship.

name: Ivan Osmak
title: Co-Founder & former CEO (stepped down October 2023; currently CEO of MeatBorsa)
sector: Enterprise SaaS / AI-powered Strategy Execution & OKR Management
stage: Acquired (by Workboard, May 2025; last independent round: Series C, December 2021, ~$120M)
hq_location: Denver, Colorado, USA (founded in Sofia, Bulgaria)
summary: Ivan Osmak is the Croatian-born co-founder and former CEO of Quantive (originally Gtmhub), a B2B AI-powered strategy execution platform he built from Sofia into a $161M-backed company before stepping down in October 2023 and going on to found MeatBorsa, a B2B meat marketplace.
suggested_action: Do not target as a portfolio investment. Osmak is a seasoned CEE-rooted founder who scaled a SaaS company to Series C ($161M raised) and a successful exit (acquisition by Workboard). Engage him as a potential LP, angel co-investor, or deal-flow source for CEE B2B SaaS and marketplace founders — his network in the Bulgarian/CEE tech ecosystem (LaunchHub-backed roots, Index Ventures/Insight Partners relationships) is highly relevant to the fund's thesis.

name: Markus Villig
title: Founder & CEO
sector: Shared Mobility / Super-App (ride-hailing, micromobility, food & grocery delivery, car-sharing)
stage: Late-stage / Post-Series E; most recent financing was a €220M syndicated revolving credit facility (May 2024)
summary: Markus Villig is the Estonian billionaire founder and CEO of Bolt, whom he bootstrapped at age 19 and scaled into a global mobility super-app serving 200M+ users across 50+ countries, making him Europe's youngest unicorn founder.
suggested_action: Markus Villig is not a target company for the fund — Bolt is a late-stage decacorn ($8.4B–$11B valuation) well beyond pre-seed scope. Approach him as a potential LP, angel co-investor, or dealflow source: he has deep CEE founder networks, has recently shown interest in Estonian defence tech, and his brand carries significant signal value for the fund's LP base.

name: Daniel Dines
title: Co-Founder & CEO
sector: Enterprise Automation / Robotic Process Automation (RPA) / Agentic AI
summary: Daniel Dines is the Romanian-born co-founder and CEO of UiPath, a publicly listed enterprise automation and AI platform company he built from a bootstrapped Bucharest startup into a $35B-valued global leader with over $1.7B in annual revenue.
suggested_action: Do not target as a portfolio company — UiPath is a public NYSE-listed unicorn. Approach Daniel Dines as a potential LP, angel co-investor, or dealflow source, particularly for CEE-origin deep tech and automation startups, leveraging his Romanian roots and personal angel activity across 24+ startups.

name: Florin Talpes
title: Co-Founder & CEO
stage: Private / Growth (PE-backed; Vitruvian Partners holds minority stake; total raised ~$7M in early rounds)
hq_location: Bucharest, Romania (dual HQ with San Antonio, Texas)
summary: Florin Talpes co-founded Bitdefender in 2001 and has grown it into a global cybersecurity leader with €330M in 2024 revenue, serving millions of consumers and thousands of enterprises worldwide.
suggested_action: Bitdefender is a large, mature private company far beyond the fund's investment scope. However, Talpes is a prominent CEE tech founder and ecosystem builder (co-founder of ANIS Romania) and Bitdefender has its own CVC (Voyager Ventures, investing seed-to-Series A in cybersecurity/AI in Europe). Approach Talpes as a potential LP, angel co-investor, or strategic deal-flow partner — particularly for cybersecurity and deep-tech deals in the CEE region.
